quotes.text: The gap between what a designer hands off and what a developer builds is where user experience goes to die.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Accessibility isn't a nice-to-have. It's the law and basic human decency. Pick whichever motivates you.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best UI component library is the one that gets out of your way. The worst is the one you have to fight.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: There are two hard things in computer science: cache invalidation, naming things, and off-by-one errors.
quotes.author: Leon Bambrick

quotes.text: It works on my machine. Ship my machine.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: I don't have a backup plan. I have 'recreate it worse from memory' plan.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: A monorepo is just a repo that gave up on boundaries. A polyrepo is just a monorepo that gave up on simplicity.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The cloud is just someone else's computer that charges you rent and occasionally catches fire.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best code review comment is 'LGTM' after 0 seconds of review. The most honest is 'I'll look at this tomorrow' repeated 47 times.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: A SQL query walks into a bar, sees two tables, and asks: 'Can I JOIN you?'
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: There are 10 types of people in the world: those who understand binary and those who don't. And those who expected base-3.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The first 90% of the code accounts for the first 90% of the development time. The remaining 10% accounts for the other 90%.
quotes.author: Tom Cargill

quotes.text: If debugging is the process of removing bugs, then programming must be the process of putting them in.
quotes.author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

quotes.text: Programming is 10% writing code and 90% understanding why it doesn't work.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The code works. Don't touch it. I don't know why it works. Don't ask.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Premature optimization is the root of all evil. But premature abstraction is the root of all suffering.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every Junior Dev: 'I'll just use a regex for this.' Every Senior Dev: 'I'll just use a regex for this.' Every Staff Dev: 'I'll just use a regex for this.' None of them have learned.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best error message is the one that never appears. The second best tells me what I did wrong, not what the code did wrong.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every time you write a comment explaining 'why', a senior dev somewhere nods. Every time you write a comment explaining 'what', a senior dev somewhere drinks.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your code needs comments to be understood, rewrite the code. If your code needs comments to be maintained, document the architecture.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to fix a bug is during code review. The second best time is never because you shipped it to production and nobody noticed.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Clean code always looks like it was written by someone who cares. Messy code looks like it was written by someone who's about to quit.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The only valid measurement of code quality is how many times you have to read it to understand it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Technical debt is what you call it when you want to feel sophisticated about procrastinating.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: A 10x developer isn't someone who codes 10x faster. It's someone who creates 10x less work for everyone else.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The worst part about being a senior developer isn't the complexity. It's knowing that the simple solution you proposed was right all along.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Rewriting from scratch is always tempting. It's also always a terrible idea. But you won't believe me until you've done it yourself.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The problem with Object-Oriented Programming is that it was invented by academics, and everything they do is overcomplicated.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every framework is somebody's way of saying 'I disagree with how you write code' and then charging you for the privilege.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best database schema is the one that answers your questions without making you write a 47-line SQL query.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Unit tests don't prevent bugs. They prevent the specific bugs you anticipated. Production finds the rest.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you're writing boilerplate, you're not programming. You're typing. Find a better abstraction or a better language.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to choose your database was yesterday. The second best time is before you have 10,000 users, not after.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: TypeScript is just JavaScript wearing a trench coat and fake mustache pretending to be a serious language.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best API is one where you don't need the docs. The second best is docs that are actually complete. Neither exists.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every good abstraction starts as 'this is so simple!' and ends as 'I can't believe how complex this is.'
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your microservices aren't microservices. They're just modules that hate each other and communicate via HTTP.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to set up CI/CD was when you started the project. The second best time is now. The worst time is after your third 'it works on my machine' incident.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your README has more badges than content, your project is a resume builder, not software.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every dev who says 'I don't need version control' has a story that starts with 'So I accidentally deleted production and...'
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Code that's easy to delete is better than code that's easy to write.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best code is no code at all. Every line of code is a liability.
quotes.author: Jeff Atwood

quotes.text: Simplicity is a prerequisite for reliability.
quotes.author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

quotes.text: The function of good software is to make the complex appear to be simple.
quotes.author: Grady Booch

quotes.text: First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.
quotes.author: John Johnson

quotes.text: Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand.
quotes.author: Martin Fowler

quotes.text: The most dangerous phrase in the language is: 'We've always done it this way.'
quotes.author: Grace Hopper

quotes.text: Ship, then test. Not test, then ship. The market doesn't wait for your test suite.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to refactor is when the code is fresh in your mind. The worst time is when you're debugging a production incident at 3 AM.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every codebase is one junior dev away from a complete rewrite.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: You can't refactor what you can't read. You can't read what you can't understand. Start there.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The difference between a senior and a junior dev isn't knowledge. It's knowing which shortcuts will haunt you later.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your test suite takes more than 5 minutes, people won't run it. If people don't run it, it doesn't exist.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers aren't the ones who write the most code. They're the ones who delete the most code.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Legacy code is just code that nobody has the courage to delete yet.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Naming things is easy. Naming things well is a skill. Naming things in a way that still makes sense 2 years later is a superpower.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best architects are the ones who can draw boundaries, not the ones who can draw boxes.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Stop building for scale you don't have. You're not Google. You have 47 users and 3 of them are your mom.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best technology choice is the one your team knows. The second best is the one that's actually right. Usually they're different.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your system design document should fit on one page. If it doesn't, your system design is too complicated.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The most expensive part of software isn't the code. It's the maintenance. Plan for it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you can't explain it to a six-year-old, you don't understand it. If your code can't be read by a junior dev, you haven't simplified it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every database migration is a small act of violence against your future self. Write the rollback first.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The worst kind of technical debt is the kind you don't know you have.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Deploy on Friday if you want. Just don't deploy on Friday afternoon and then leave.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best alerting system is one that pages you about things that actually matter. The worst is one that pages you about everything so you ignore it all.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your staging environment should be a copy of production. If it's not, it's a lie you tell yourself.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Kubernetes is what happens when sysadmins get bored and decide to turn 'turn it off and on again' into a distributed system.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Docker: because 'it works on my machine' stopped being acceptable. Now it works on everyone's machine but nobody understands why.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you need a PhD to understand your deployment pipeline, you've built a research project, not a product.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Monitoring tells you what's broken. Observability tells you why. Most teams have neither.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best incident response is the one where you don't need to respond because the system healed itself. The second best is one with a runbook.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Post-mortems are like therapy: everyone feels better afterward, but nothing actually changes.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: API rate limits aren't a technical constraint. They're a business model disguised as engineering.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Multi-tenant architecture is great until one customer's query takes down everyone else's Saturday.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to build a CLI version of your SaaS is when your power users ask for it. They're your best advocates.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: API-first SaaS companies think in integrations. API-last SaaS companies think in features. Guess which one wins.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best tech stack for an indie project is the one you can ship with today. Not the one you'll learn eventually.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The problem with requirements is that they're written by people who don't have to implement them.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every 'easy' feature request is just code someone else doesn't have to write.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Edge cases aren't edge cases when you have enough users. They're Tuesday.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The user who says 'it's just a simple feature' has never written a line of code in their life.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best feature is the one you don't build. Every feature is a future maintenance burden and a current cognitive load.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Happy path development is what you do when you've never met a user. Users don't take happy paths. They take confused paths.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best code you'll ever write is the code you delete.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every time you copy-paste code, a unit test dies.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Debugging is like being a detective in a crime movie where you're also the suspect.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your code works, don't touch it. If it doesn't work, maybe also don't touch it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best architecture is the one that doesn't need an architecture diagram.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: A function that does one thing well is worth a thousand functions that do everything poorly.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Git blame isn't about finding who's responsible. It's about finding who's qualified to explain what this code was supposed to do.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best PR is the smallest one that solves the problem. The best code review is the one that actually happens.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Write code as if the person maintaining it is a violent psychopath who knows where you live.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The only constant in software development is the changing of requirements.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you can't explain your architecture in 3 sentences, you don't understand it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to learn about security is before you get hacked. The second best time is after. The worst time is never.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every programmer has a story about the time they spent 8 hours debugging a missing semicolon. If they say they don't, they're lying.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to learn a new language is when you need it. The worst is when you think it'll make you a better programmer.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best developers aren't the ones who write the most clever code. They're the ones who write the most boring code that works.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your code is a love letter to the future developer who maintains it. Write it like you care about their mental health.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your CSS file is over 500 lines, you're either doing something wrong or you're building Bootstrap.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers write the fewest lines of code. The worst engineers write the most features.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you can't test it, you can't ship it. If you can't ship it, you can't charge for it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to think about scalability is when you have 100 users. Not when you have 100 million.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your database schema is the foundation of your house. Build it wrong and everything else crumbles.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best error handling is the kind that never fires. The second best is the kind that fires with a helpful message.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you're debugging for more than an hour, stop. Walk away. The answer is usually something obvious you're too tired to see.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best security practice is assuming you've already been breached.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every new dependency is a potential vulnerability. Audit them like you audit your employees.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to think about performance is never. The second best time is when users complain.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Caching is the answer to everything until it's the source of everything that's broken.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers leave code better than they found it. The worst leave it worse than they found it. Most leave it the same.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every production incident is a lesson. If you're not learning from them, you're just collecting them.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to set up backups is before you need them. The second best time is immediately after you needed them.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you can't describe your deployment process in 3 steps, it's too complicated.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers don't have fewer bugs. They catch them earlier. Shift left, not right.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your CI pipeline is your safety net. If it's slow, developers skip it. If they skip it, you're shipping bugs.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Code reviews aren't about finding bugs. They're about spreading knowledge and maintaining standards.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to think about data migration is during schema design. Not after you've got 10TB of data.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your logging should tell a story. If your logs are noise, your monitoring is useless.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers don't write more code. They write less code that does more.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you don't have a type system, your tests are your type system. If you have both, you're just being thorough.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to improve your code is to delete half of it. Then delete half again.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every codebase has that one file that nobody dares to touch. That file is your biggest risk.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers are the ones who make other engineers better. Write code that teaches.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your feature flags are more complex than your code, you've built a configuration management system.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every line of code is a liability. Every feature is a bet. Every deploy is a risk. Ship accordingly.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your README is your first impression. If it's bad, nobody will find out how good your code is.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers are lazy in the right ways: lazy about doing things twice, not lazy about doing things right.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every production database needs a backup. Every backup needs a test. Every test needs a schedule.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to refactor is when nobody is watching. The second best time is between features. The worst time is during an incident.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your GitHub profile is your resume. If it's empty, so is your argument for why you're a good developer.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to learn is to teach. The best way to teach is to build something that explains itself.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to get better at coding is to code. Not to watch tutorials. Not to read books. To code.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you're not embarrassed by your code from 6 months ago, you're not growing.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: API-first isn't a technical decision. It's a business decision. It means your product can live everywhere.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to think about internationalization is when you're building your first date picker. Not after you've hardcoded American dates everywhere.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every SaaS should have an API. Every API should have docs. Every docs page should have examples.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best API design is one where the next endpoint is exactly where you'd expect it to be.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: REST APIs are just CRUD with better marketing.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: GraphQL is what happens when you tell frontend developers they can have exactly what they want. They want everything.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best architecture is the one that lets you swap out any component without the rest of the system noticing.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Event-driven architecture is great until you have to debug why an event from 3 days ago triggered a cascade of failures.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best message queue is the one that never loses messages. The second best is the one that's easy to debug when it does.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your database is your system's memory. Treat it with respect. Backup it. Index it. Never let it forget.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to scale is horizontally. The best way to stay sane is vertically. Choose wisely.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your CI pipeline should be faster than your coffee break. If it's not, optimize it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers write code that writes code. The second best write code. The worst write comments.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your database has more than 50 tables, you probably need a domain expert, not a better ORM.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to add caching is when profiling tells you to. Not when you think it might help.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every system has a bottleneck. Find it. Fix it. Then find the next one.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers know that premature abstraction is worse than premature optimization.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your code review should take less than 30 minutes. If it takes longer, the PR is too big.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers don't just write code. They write the tools that help others write code.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every database query should have an index. Every index should have a reason. Every reason should be tested.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to think about security is before you ship. The second best time is after you've been breached.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your test suite is your safety net. If it's full of holes, you'll fall through.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers know the difference between clever code and clear code. They choose clear.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your API versioning should be boring and predictable. If it's exciting, something's wrong.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Perfection is achieved not when there is nothing more to add, but when there is nothing left to take away.
quotes.author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

quotes.text: Make it work, make it right, make it fast.
quotes.author: Kent Beck

quotes.text: The only way to go fast is to go well.
quotes.author: Robert C. Martin

quotes.text: Programs must be written for people to read, and only incidentally for machines to execute.
quotes.author: Harold Abelson

quotes.text: The purpose of software engineering is to control complexity, not to create it.
quotes.author: Barbara Liskov

quotes.text: Simplicity does not precede complexity, but follows it.
quotes.author: Alan Perlis

quotes.text: Measuring programming progress by lines of code is like measuring aircraft building progress by weight.
quotes.author: Bill Gates

quotes.text: The most important property of a program is whether it accomplishes the intention of its user.
quotes.author: C.A.R. Hoare

quotes.text: Worse is better.
quotes.author: Richard P. Gabriel

quotes.text: The computer was born to solve problems that did not exist before.
quotes.author: Bill Gates

quotes.text: Controlling complexity is the essence of computer programming.
quotes.author: Brian Kernighan

quotes.text: The most dangerous thing in the world is a programmer who thinks they understand security.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers are the ones who know what not to build.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best code is no code at all. The second best code is code that writes itself.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every system is perfectly designed to get the results it gets.
quotes.author: W. Edwards Deming

quotes.text: The best on-call rotation is the one that never fires. The second best is the one that fires at reasonable hours.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your deployment process requires a runbook, it's too complicated. Simplify it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to add health checks is before you need them. Not during an incident.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Rollback is not a failure. Rollback is a feature. Use it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best on-call engineers are the ones who fix the problem AND the alert. Not just the problem.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every production incident should result in exactly two things: a fix and a better alert.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best monitoring tells you what's broken. The best observability tells you why. Most teams have neither.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your SLA should be honest. If you can't hit 99.9%, don't promise it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best incident response is one where you don't panic. Panic is the enemy of resolution.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your staging environment should match production. If it doesn't, your staging is lying to you.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product should work without JavaScript. Not because JavaScript is bad, but because resilience is good.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to handle errors is to prevent them. The second best is to catch them early. The third is to show a useful message.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best debugging tool is a good night's sleep. Most bugs look obvious in the morning.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you've been debugging for more than an hour, stop. Walk away. The answer is usually something obvious.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to find a bug is to write a test that reproduces it. Not to stare at the code until it confesses.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Debugging is detective work. You look for clues, form theories, and test them. Sometimes the culprit is you.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best bug reports are the ones that include steps to reproduce. The worst are 'it doesn't work.'
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your test suite should catch regressions. If it doesn't, it's just running code for no reason.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers are the ones who can debug without looking at the code. They understand the system, not just the syntax.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every bug has a root cause. Find it. Don't just fix the symptom.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers don't just fix bugs. They write tests that prevent them from coming back.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you can reproduce it, you can fix it. If you can't reproduce it, it doesn't exist.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to prevent bugs is to write simple code. Simple code has fewer places to hide bugs.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your logging should be detailed enough to debug, but not so detailed that it drowns in noise.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to add logging is before you need it. The second best time is during the incident you're having.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your error logs are empty, your logging is broken. Errors happen. Capture them.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best debugging strategy is to add logging, not to add breakpoints. Logging scales. Breakpoints don't.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your stack trace is your best friend. Read it. Understand it. It's trying to help you.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every production bug is a missed test. Write the test first next time.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to fix a bug is to write a test that reproduces it, then fix the code until the test passes.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Data is the new oil. But like oil, it's useless until you refine it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your data pipeline is only as good as its weakest transformation. Garbage in, garbage out.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: My code doesn't have bugs. It has 'random features.'
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: I don't always test my code, but when I do, I do it in production.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Code never lies. Comments sometimes do.
quotes.author: Ron Jeffries

quotes.text: There are only 10 types of people in the world: those who understand binary and those who don't.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: A programmer's wife tells him: 'Go to the store and buy a loaf of bread. If they have eggs, buy a dozen.' He comes home with 12 loaves.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Why do programmers prefer dark mode? Because light attracts bugs.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: What's the object-oriented way to become wealthy? Inheritance.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Why do Java developers wear glasses? Because they can't C#.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Programming today is a race between software engineers striving to build bigger and better idiot-proof programs, and the Universe trying to produce bigger and better idiots. So far, the Universe is winning.
quotes.author: Rick Cook

quotes.text: The best thing about a Boolean is even if you are wrong, you are only off by a bit.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Hardware: the parts of a computer system that can be kicked.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The computer is incredibly fast, accurate, and stupid. Man is incredibly slow, inaccurate, and brilliant. The marriage of the two is a force beyond calculation.
quotes.author: Leo Cherne

quotes.text: There's no place like 127.0.0.1.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The Internet? Is that thing still around?
quotes.author: Homer Simpson

quotes.text: It's not a bug. It's an undocumented feature.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Have you tried turning it off and on again?
quotes.author: Roy (IT Crowd)

quotes.text: The best way to get a flat tire is to not have a spare.
quotes.author: Murphy

quotes.text: The best error message is the one you never see. The second best is one that makes you laugh.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best IDE is the one that disappears. You shouldn't be thinking about the tool.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to learn React is to build something with it. Not to read about it. Build.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Tailwind CSS is just inline styles with extra steps. And that's fine.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best database is the one you know best. Not the one that's trending on Hacker News.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Rust is what happens when C++ developers get tired of segfaults. It's also what happens when they get tired of productivity.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best frontend framework is the one your team knows. The second best is the one that fits the problem.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Git is the best version control system. That's not saying much, but it's still true.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best Python is the Python you don't write. Use libraries.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best JavaScript is the JavaScript you don't write. Use TypeScript instead.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Docker is just a fancy way of saying 'it works on my machine' and then shipping your machine.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best API is the one you don't need to build. Use a service.
quotes.author: based
